source_jobs.title: Skilled Residential Handyman (Redwood City)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CASA

We believe owning a home should feel as special as the moments that take place within them. If our mission inspires you, we'd love to hear from you.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a skilled and reliable handyman to join our private property management team. Ideal candidate will work closely with us to provide high-quality handyman, repair, and proactive maintenance services for our member's primary homes. You should be friendly, professional, and comfortable doing basic handyman work.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Proactive maintenance – you'll keep major appliances and home systems maintained on an established schedule. This might include replacing filters, checking batteries, and other similar tasks to keep the home running well

 - Home repairs – you'll mount artwork, patch drywall, touch-up paint, adjust fixtures, and other similar tasks that come up in the home. Your caulk lines should be clean and crisp!

 - Basic electrical & plumbing, like installing light fixtures & thermostats, replacing light switches, troubleshooting outlets, installing faucets, clearing clogged drains, and the like

 - Other property management tasks, like picking up packages, taking in the mail while a household is out on vacation, hanging holiday lights, or filling car tires with air when needed




QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience in a handyman, property maintenance, or technician role, ideally in residential homes is a plus

 - Strong knowledge of repair and maintenance techniques. Quality and attention to detail is a priority

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills – you'll be interacting with homeowners and need to be clear, friendly, helpful, and professional.

 - You have your own general tools, and can use them safely and effectively

 - You have your own vehicle, and can reliably travel around the San Mateo County area

 - A clean background check and drivers license


ADDITIONAL INFO

 - This is a contract hourly role to start ($40/hour), approximately 10-40 hours.

 - The hours are flexible around your schedule. Looking for weekdays, weekends, and/ or early evenings.

 - You'll be supported – scheduling, communication w/ homeowners, and parts/materials ordering is handled for you, so you can focus on the work to be done.

 - Service Areas: Alameda & Contra Costa (open to San Francisco, San Mateo, Santa Clara, Marin county candidates as well)


INTERESTED?

 - Apply to this role with your name, contact info, and resume

 - Let us know why you'd be a great fit

 - Check us out at getcasa.com http://getcasa.com

source_jobs.title: In-Home Inventory Specialist (San Fernando Valley)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CASA

We believe owning a home should feel as special as the moments that take place within them. If our mission inspires you, we'd love to hear from you.


ABOUT THE ROLE

As an In-Home Onboarding Specialist, you’ll play a foundational role in onboarding new members — capturing the details of their home so Casa can take it from there. Your professionalism, presence, and attention to detail will directly shape the member’s first impression.

You’ll be the friendly, polished face of Casa during onboarding visits. Together with a teammate, you’ll visit member homes to inventory appliances, lighting, safety systems, paint, and more — creating a complete digital profile of the home.

This role blends hospitality, logistics, and light fieldwork. You’ll use our specialized app and equipment to document everything cleanly and accurately, ensuring the home is ready for ongoing service.

This is a mobile, field-based role. You must be comfortable entering private spaces, interacting with high-end clients, and operating responsibly and discreetly inside their homes.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Conduct detailed, room-by-room inventories of residential homes

 - Photograph appliances, electronics, lighting fixtures, and other home assets

 - Use a camera to create 3D scans and floorplans (training provided)

 - Use a paint scanner to log exact wall colors and finishes

 - Log utility locations, fire safety devices, and other essential home infrastructure

 - Speak with homeowners to gather context, vendors, and preferences

 - Represent Casa with professionalism, courtesy, and discretion at all times

 - Handle equipment carefully and document all work accurately in our app




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - At least 2 years of experience in one or more of the following: home services, concierge, logistics, facilities, high-end customer service, or related fields

 - Excellent communication and interpersonal skills

 - Detail-oriented, organized, and able to follow structured workflows

 - Comfortable working in others’ homes with professionalism and discretion

 - Ability to lift and carry equipment, move through multi-level homes, and perform hands-on tasks

 - Must have a reliable personal vehicle, valid driver’s license, and proof of insurance

 - Tech-savvy: comfortable using apps, iPad, smartphone camera, Bluetooth-connected tools

 - Clear background check required




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience in property management, real estate, home inventory, in-home services, or inspection/insurance assessments

 - Hospitality or white-glove service background




COMPENSATION & PERKS

 - $25/hour

 - Flexible weekday/weekend scheduling (10–30 hours/week depending on availability)

 - Professional equipment, training, and app access provided

 - Opportunities to grow with a fast-moving startup
   
   Check us out at getcasa.com http://getcasa.com

source_jobs.title: Remote Estimator, Home Services
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CASA

We believe owning a home should feel as special as the moments that take place within them. If our mission inspires you, we'd love to hear from you.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're hiring a Remote Estimator to define and scope handyman jobs before they reach the field. This is a high-judgment, critical role where you own the front-end definition of work by reviewing requests (photos/descriptions) and translating them into clear, actionable scopes. The accuracy of your decisions directly impacts job success, cost, and homeowner trust. You will also interact with homeowners to clarify details and ensure the job is properly prepared. You must work closely with operations and field teams, and speed, accuracy, and strong judgment are essential. If scoping is done well, the downstream process works; if not, everything breaks. This is an hourly position requiring approximately 40 hours per week across a consistent 5-day schedule that includes one weekend day.




WHAT YOU’LL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR

 - Review incoming job requests including photos, videos, and descriptions and define clear, complete scopes of work

 - Determine required tasks, materials, tools, and estimated time needed for each job

 - Ensure scopes are specific enough to be executed without additional clarification

 - Identify missing or unclear information and proactively follow up with homeowners to fill gaps

 - Communicate with homeowners to clarify requests, set expectations, and prepare them for upcoming visits

 - Provide clear, professional, and timely communication to homeowners throughout the pre-visit process

 - Flag jobs that are out of scope, unsafe, or require licensed specialists

 - Maintain consistency in scoping standards across all job types

 - Balance speed and accuracy and scope jobs quickly without sacrificing quality

 - Incorporate field feedback to continuously improve scoping accuracy

 - Reduce job failure, rework, and scope creep through better upfront definition

 - Maintain clean documentation of scopes, notes, and assumptions

 - Support operations with queue management and prioritization of incoming requests

 - Identify patterns in mis-scoped jobs and improve guidelines to prevent repeat issues




ABOUT YOU

You are a practitioner first. You have done the work yourself in handyman services, construction, maintenance, or a related trade and understand what it actually takes to complete a job. You can look at a few photos and quickly understand what is really going on. You communicate clearly and confidently with homeowners. You know how to ask the right questions, guide conversations, and set expectations in a way that builds trust.

You make practical decisions, not theoretical ones. You move fast but do not cut corners. You understand that missing a small detail upfront can create major problems later. You take ownership of getting the scope right the first time. You are comfortable working independently, making judgment calls, and being accountable for outcomes.

You do not need corporate experience. You need:

 - Strong trade intuition

 - Attention to detail

 - Bias toward action

 - Clear and structured thinking

 - Strong customer communication skills




MUST HAVES

 - 2 or more years of hands-on experience in handyman work, construction, maintenance, or a related trade

 - Strong ability to assess jobs remotely using photos, videos, and written descriptions

 - Experience communicating directly with customers or homeowners

 - Working knowledge of common home repair tasks, materials, tools, and time requirements

 - Ability to break down work into clear, actionable scopes

 - High attention to detail and accuracy

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills

 - Comfort working in a fast-paced, high-volume environment

 - Ability to manage multiple jobs simultaneously without losing quality

 - Reliable and responsive and does not let work sit or fall through the cracks

 - Ability to work Pacific business hours

source_jobs.title: Founding Product Marketer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CASA

We believe owning a home should feel as special as the moments that take place within them. If our mission inspires you, we'd love to hear from you.




THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Founding Product Marketer to be Casa’s storyteller-in-chief. You’ll be the first person at Casa focused entirely on product marketing, defining how we communicate our value to the world and show up everywhere — shaping the messaging, positioning, and creative that help homeowners understand what Casa is, why it matters, how it fits into their life at home, and why it’s worth becoming a member.

This role is equal parts storytelling and conversion: defining what a new kind of home ownership experience is and why it matters, while crafting the copy and creative that make someone stop, pay attention, and act. You’ll own our narrative end to end — reimagining how Casa communicates value across every surface, and packaging Casa’s growing set of benefits into stories that land with the right people at the right moments.

This is a highly hands-on role with significant ownership and autonomy. You’ll work directly with our CEO to define strategy, and execute independently. We’re looking for a senior individual contributor, not a manager.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - Narrative & positioning: own how we package and explain Casa — both the overarching story and the messaging for each individual benefit (package pickups, recurring services, and more), tailored to the people each one resonates with

 - Collateral & creative for everything we build: compelling assets across every channel and touchpoint — paid ads, app store, social, in-person, product launches

 - Voice & tone: define and drive Casa’s voice and tone from a commercial context, so the brand stays clear, consistent, and resonant everywhere

 - Conversion: own the gap between first impression and signup — pinpoint exactly where and why prospective members hesitate, and sharpen the messaging and experience that move them from interested to member

 - Creative direction: bring campaigns and assets to life — sometimes in collaboration with external partners and agencies — always ensuring the work reflects Casa’s story

 - Measurement: define what good looks like, and translate insight from customers and channels into stronger messaging and positioning




WHO YOU ARE

 - 8+ years in product marketing, brand marketing, or messaging & positioning roles for consumer products

 - An exceptional writer — you craft messaging that is clear, emotionally resonant, persuasive, and conversion-oriented

 - You own narrative end to end: you can take a product, understand what people care about, and distill a compelling story

 - You think deeply about consumer psychology and decision-making – what makes someone stop, trust, and act, not just click

 - You’ve marketed a high-consideration, personal purchase — like home, family, or lifestyle services — where trust, clarity, and storytelling materially influence the decision

 - You combine creativity with analytical rigor; you can talk copy, funnel conversion, and creative performance in the same conversation

 - Comfortable collaborating with external partners and agencies when it helps bring the work to life

 - Highly self-motivated, resourceful, and low-ego; you operate with significant ownership and don’t wait to be unblocked




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience marketing premium consumer, subscription, home, hospitality, or lifestyle brands

 - Experience reimagining how a product is presented and explained around a clearer, more compelling story

 - Early-stage startup experience building messaging and systems from scratch

source_jobs.title: Senior iOS Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CASA

We believe owning a home should feel as special as the moments that take place within them. If our mission inspires you, we'd love to hear from you.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re hiring a founding Senior iOS Engineer to help us design, develop, and ship best-in-class mobile applications that deliver exceptional value to our members.

You’ll work closely with our founders, fellow engineers, and designers to create thoughtful and groundbreaking products that deliver exceptional value to our members. You’ll help build our core systems and product and define much of our founding team culture.




SOME OF THE RESPONSIBILITIES YOU’LL HAVE

 - Lead the architecture and development of our iOS applications, ensuring a seamless user experience for homeowners and vendors

 - Prototype new features and applications to quickly respond to user needs and feedback

 - Collaborate closely with designers to create intuitive and beautiful user interfaces

 - Ensure the performance, quality, and responsiveness of our applications through continuous testing and refinement

 - Stay current with the latest advancements in iOS development, and integrate new technologies and methodologies as appropriate

 - Level up a growing engineering team by reviewing code and designs of teammates and providing constructive feedback to maintain high quality




ABOUT YOU

 - You’re well experienced. 5+ years in iOS development, with a strong track record of building and shipping mobile applications and deep proficiency in Swift and Apple SDKs/APIs

 - You’re passionate about design and user experiences. You enjoy collaborating with designers to ship beautiful, functional apps that users love

 - You’re excited to be a founding engineer at an early-stage startup. This means that there’s a lot of systems and structure yet to be put in place, and you’ll play an active role in building and shaping those alongside our company culture

 - You’re excited to build. You embrace the challenges and growth that come with joining an early-stage company, thriving in a fast-moving, evolving environment

 - You think and act like an owner. You take a long-term view, make decisions with care, and are eager to take initiative and responsibility with good judgment

 - You roll up your sleeves. You find satisfaction in taking action, solving problems, and learning quickly, rather than delegating or managing from the sidelines

 - You’re mission-driven and adaptable. You understand the stakes of a startup, focus on what matters most, and dedicate yourself to building for long-term success

 - You thrive on collaboration and problem-solving. You work best in an in-person culture, feeding off shared energy, brainstorming, and engaging with others daily

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CASA

We believe owning a home should feel as special as the moments that take place within them. If our mission inspires you, we'd love to hear from you.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re hiring a Senior Product Designer to craft best-in-class products that solve real-world problems facing homeowners.

You’ll work closely with our founders, fellow engineers, and designers to create thoughtful and groundbreaking products that deliver exceptional value to our members. You'll help build our core systems and product and define much of our team culture.




SOME OF THE RESPONSIBILITIES YOU’LL HAVE

 - Lead the design process for our iOS apps, ensuring a seamless user experience for members and vendors

 - Prototype and iterate on new features to quickly respond to user needs and feedback

 - Collaborate closely with engineers to create intuitive, beautiful, and functional user interfaces that bring our product vision to life




ABOUT YOU

 - You’re well experienced. 5+ years in product design, with a strong track record of designing and shipping mobile apps, and a deep proficiency in Figma

 - You’re passionate about user-centered design. You care deeply about crafting intuitive, delightful user experiences and have a portfolio that demonstrates your design thinking and process

 - You’re excited to be a founding designer at an early-stage startup. This means that there’s a lot of systems and structure yet to be put in place, and you’ll play an active role in building and shaping those alongside our company culture

 - You thrive in fast-moving, unstructured environments. We move quickly, and we’re looking for someone who is excited to set the design direction and increase our pace. (If you’re looking for the stability and structure that comes along with a more established company, this probably isn’t the right time)

 - You’re an owner, not a renter. You always take a long-term view when making design decisions, and consider the company’s best interests as if they were your own. We’re looking for folks who are excited to take initiative, be trusted with broad responsibility, and who have the good judgment necessary for it

 - You’re ready to roll up your sleeves and get to work. We’re not looking for managers or people who want to delegate at this time. We’re looking for folks who get satisfaction from creating beautiful designs, getting things done, and learning quickly along the way

 - You’re at your best around others. We’re intentionally building an in-person culture to feed off of each other’s energy, collaborate and brainstorm together, and get the fulfillment that comes with healthy interactions with coworkers on a daily basis

 - You’re mission-oriented and stage-aware. Startups are fragile, and you know that every decision we make increases or decreases the odds of our company’s success. You’re ready to dedicate your time and attention at work to solving our business challenges, not to address political or societal issues

source_jobs.title: Skilled Residential Handyman (San Francisco)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CASA

We believe owning a home should feel as special as the moments that take place within them. If our mission inspires you, we'd love to hear from you.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a skilled and reliable handyman to join our private property management team. Ideal candidate will work closely with us to provide high-quality handyman, repair, and proactive maintenance services for our member's primary homes. You should be friendly, professional, and comfortable doing basic handyman work.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Proactive maintenance – you'll keep major appliances and home systems maintained on an established schedule. This might include replacing filters, checking batteries, and other similar tasks to keep the home running well

 - Home repairs – you'll mount artwork, patch drywall, touch-up paint, adjust fixtures, and other similar tasks that come up in the home. Your caulk lines should be clean and crisp!

 - Basic electrical & plumbing, like installing light fixtures & thermostats, replacing light switches, troubleshooting outlets, installing faucets, clearing clogged drains, and the like

 - Other property management tasks, like picking up packages, taking in the mail while a household is out on vacation, hanging holiday lights, or filling car tires with air when needed




QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience in a handyman, property maintenance, or technician role, ideally in residential homes is a plus

 - Strong knowledge of repair and maintenance techniques. Quality and attention to detail is a priority

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills – you'll be interacting with homeowners and need to be clear, friendly, helpful, and professional.

 - You have your own general tools, and can use them safely and effectively

 - You have your own vehicle, and can reliably travel around San Francisco

 - A clean background check and drivers license


ADDITIONAL INFO

 - This is a contract hourly role to start ($40/hour), approximately 10-40 hours a week.

 - The hours are flexible around your schedule. Looking for weekdays, weekends, and/ or early evenings.

 - You'll be supported – scheduling, communication w/ homeowners, and parts/materials ordering is handled for you, so you can focus on the work to be done.

 - Service Areas: San Francisco (open to Alameda, Contra Costa, San Mateo, Santa Clara, Marin county candidates as well)


INTERESTED?

 - Apply to this role with your name, contact info, and resume

 - Let us know why you'd be a great fit

 - Check us out at getcasa.com http://getcasa.com



#ZR

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CASA

We believe owning a home should feel as special as the moments that take place within them. If our mission inspires you, we'd love to hear from you.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re hiring a founding Senior Backend Engineer to help us design, develop, and ship robust backend systems that deliver exceptional value to our members.

You’ll work closely with our founders, fellow engineers, and designers to create thoughtful and groundbreaking products that deliver exceptional value to our members. You’ll help build our core systems and product and define much of our founding team culture.




SOME OF THE RESPONSIBILITIES YOU’LL HAVE

 - Lead the architecture and development of our backend systems, ensuring they are scalable, secure, and reliable

 - Design and implement APIs that power our iOS applications and other client interfaces

 - Optimize performance and scalability of our services to handle a growing number of members and data

 - Stay current with the latest advancements in backend technologies and cloud infrastructure, and integrate new technologies and methodologies as appropriate

 - Level up a growing engineering team by reviewing code and designs of teammates and providing constructive feedback to maintain high quality




ABOUT YOU

 - You’re passionate about building robust systems. You have a deep understanding of backend architecture, API design, and database management, and you’re excited to build systems that scale with our growing member base

 - You’re excited to be a founding engineer at an early-stage startup. This means that there’s a lot of systems and structure yet to be put in place, and you’ll play an active role in building and shaping those alongside our company culture

 - You’re excited to build. You embrace the challenges and growth that come with joining an early-stage company, thriving in a fast-moving, evolving environment

 - You think and act like an owner. You take a long-term view, make decisions with care, and are eager to take initiative and responsibility with good judgment

 - You roll up your sleeves. You find satisfaction in taking action, solving problems, and learning quickly, rather than delegating or managing from the sidelines

 - You’re mission-driven and adaptable. You understand the stakes of a startup, focus on what matters most, and dedicate yourself to building for long-term success

 - You thrive on collaboration and problem-solving. You work best in an in-person culture, feeding off shared energy, brainstorming, and engaging with others daily

source_jobs.title: In-Home Inventory Specialist (Marin)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CASA

We believe owning a home should feel as special as the moments that take place within them. If our mission inspires you, we'd love to hear from you.


ABOUT THE ROLE

As an In-Home Onboarding Specialist, you’ll play a foundational role in onboarding new members — capturing the details of their home so Casa can take it from there. Your professionalism, presence, and attention to detail will directly shape the member’s first impression.

You’ll be the friendly, polished face of Casa during onboarding visits. Together with a teammate, you’ll visit member homes to inventory appliances, lighting, safety systems, paint, and more — creating a complete digital profile of the home.

This role blends hospitality, logistics, and light fieldwork. You’ll use our specialized app and equipment to document everything cleanly and accurately, ensuring the home is ready for ongoing service.

This is a mobile, field-based role. You must be comfortable entering private spaces, interacting with high-end clients, and operating responsibly and discreetly inside their homes.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Conduct detailed, room-by-room inventories of residential homes

 - Photograph appliances, electronics, lighting fixtures, and other home assets

 - Use a camera to create 3D scans and floorplans (training provided)

 - Use a paint scanner to log exact wall colors and finishes

 - Log utility locations, fire safety devices, and other essential home infrastructure

 - Speak with homeowners to gather context, vendors, and preferences

 - Represent Casa with professionalism, courtesy, and discretion at all times

 - Handle equipment carefully and document all work accurately in our app




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - At least 2 years of experience in one or more of the following: home services, concierge, logistics, facilities, high-end customer service, or related fields

 - Excellent communication and interpersonal skills

 - Detail-oriented, organized, and able to follow structured workflows

 - Comfortable working in others’ homes with professionalism and discretion

 - Ability to lift and carry equipment, move through multi-level homes, and perform hands-on tasks

 - Must have a reliable personal vehicle, valid driver’s license, and proof of insurance

 - Tech-savvy: comfortable using apps, iPad, smartphone camera, Bluetooth-connected tools

 - Clear background check required




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience in property management, real estate, home inventory, in-home services, or inspection/insurance assessments

 - Hospitality or white-glove service background




COMPENSATION & PERKS

 - $30/hour starting rate

 - Flexible weekday/weekend scheduling (20–40 hours/week depending on availability)

 - Professional equipment, training, and app access provided

 - Opportunities to grow with a fast-moving startup
   
   Check us out at getcasa.com http://getcasa.com

source_jobs.title: Vendor Partnerships Coordinator (Home Services)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CASA

We believe owning a home should feel as special as the moments that take place within them. If our mission inspires you, we'd love to hear from you.




ABOUT THE ROLE

This is a hands-on role focused on building and maintaining Casa's vendor network.

You'll spend much of your time identifying, recruiting, onboarding, and supporting service providers across a wide range of home service categories. You'll work closely with our Concierge and Operations teams to ensure we have reliable, high-quality vendor coverage wherever our members need support.

This is not a traditional vendor management role overseeing a large established network. We're looking for someone excited to help build the systems, processes, and relationships that will allow Casa to scale.




WHAT YOU'LL DO


VENDOR SOURCING & RECRUITMENT

 - Source and recruit vendors across home service categories including plumbing, electrical, HVAC, appliance repair, landscaping, handyman services, cleaning, and more

 - Build vendor pipelines in existing and future markets

 - Conduct outreach via phone, email, referrals, and local research

 - Identify coverage gaps and proactively recruit providers to fill them

 - Support vendor acquisition efforts ahead of market launches


VENDOR ONBOARDING

 - Own the vendor onboarding process from first conversation through activation

 - Collect and verify required documentation including insurance, licenses, W9s, and agreements

 - Ensure vendors understand Casa's expectations and operating procedures

 - Improve onboarding efficiency and reduce activation time


VENDOR QUALITY & PERFORMANCE

 - Monitor vendor responsiveness, reliability, and service quality

 - Gather feedback from Concierge teams and members

 - Help develop vendor scorecards and performance tracking

 - Identify top-performing vendors and opportunities for improvement

 - Maintain accurate vendor records and profiles


PROCESS IMPROVEMENT

 - Build repeatable workflows for sourcing, onboarding, and vendor management

 - Create documentation and SOPs

 - Maintain vendor databases and operational systems

 - Partner with Operations leadership to improve scalability and efficiency




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Experience in recruiting, vendor management, property management, marketplace operations, or a related field

 - Strong organizational skills and attention to detail

 - Comfortable making outbound calls and building relationships

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - Highly proactive and resourceful

 - Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced startup environment

 - Excited to build processes rather than simply follow them




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience working with contractors, vendors, or service providers

 - Experience in home services, property management, hospitality, or local marketplaces

 - Experience launching new markets or building operational processes

 - Familiarity with CRM or vendor management systems
